rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes, avoiding implementation-level bindings in type contracts.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the AADL model.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid unresolved references.
Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and their expressions to prevent type mismatches.
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Relate visible outputs to visible inputs in guarantees; do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` with proper syntax and visible identifiers.
Structure AGREE contracts with clear labels and valid expressions for diagnostics to be actionable.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation details in component type contracts.
Do not assume AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
Implementation-level bindings and subcomponent paths in type contracts
